questions.question: Explain the difference between block-listing and allow-listing in input validation. Which one is more secure and why?
keyPoints: Block-listing denies known bad inputs, allow-listing accepts known good inputs
Allow-listing is more secure as it only permits expected data
Block-listing can be bypassed with new attack variants
Allow-listing provides defense against zero-day attacks

questions.question: What is SQL Injection? List two methods to prevent SQL Injection attacks.
keyPoints: SQL injection inserts malicious SQL code through user input
Prevention methods: parameterized queries, prepared statements, stored procedures
Input validation and escaping special characters
Using ORM frameworks

questions.question: Define Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) and explain three techniques to prevent it.
keyPoints: XSS injects malicious scripts into web pages
Prevention: input validation, output encoding
Using Content Security Policy (CSP) headers
HttpOnly and Secure cookie attributes

questions.question: What are the key principles of secure password storage? Explain why storing passwords in encrypted form is not recommended.
keyPoints: Use one-way hashing with salt
Use algorithms like bcrypt, scrypt, or Argon2
Encryption is reversible - if key is compromised, passwords are exposed
Hashing is irreversible - only password verification possible

questions.question: Explain the concept of defense in depth in the context of application security.
keyPoints: Multiple layers of security controls
If one layer fails, others provide protection
Examples: firewall, authentication, authorization, encryption, logging
Comprehensive approach covering network, application, and data security

questions.question: What is Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF)? How can it be prevented?
keyPoints: CSRF exploits authenticated user sessions
Attacker tricks user into submitting malicious requests
Prevention: anti-CSRF tokens, SameSite cookies, Referer validation
Double-submit cookie pattern

questions.question: Discuss the importance of secure error handling in application development.
keyPoints: Log detailed errors internally for debugging
Display generic error messages to users
Don't expose system paths, database structure, or stack traces
Helps prevent information disclosure attacks

questions.question: What is input validation? Discuss various input validation techniques and explain why server-side validation is essential despite client-side validation.
keyPoints: Input validation checks data before processing
Types: type checking, format validation, length validation, range checking
Allow-listing vs block-listing approaches
Client-side validation can be bypassed (disabled/modified by attacker)
Server-side validation is the last line of defense
Both should be used together for comprehensive validation

questions.question: Explain session management security. Discuss common session attacks and countermeasures to secure session handling.
keyPoints: Session management creates and maintains user sessions
Session ID generation must be unpredictable (cryptographically secure)
Session hijacking: stealing session IDs via XSS, network sniffing
Session fixation: attacker sets user session ID before authentication
Countermeasures: HttpOnly cookies, Secure flag, SameSite attribute
Session timeout, regeneration of session IDs after login
HTTPS for all authenticated connections

questions.question: Describe the secure software development lifecycle. What are the key security practices that should be incorporated at each phase?
keyPoints: Requirements: threat modeling, security requirements
Design: security architecture, threat analysis
Implementation: secure coding practices, code reviews
Testing: penetration testing, vulnerability scanning
Deployment: secure configuration, monitoring
Maintenance: patch management, incident response
Key practices: least privilege, defense in depth, input validation
Regular security audits and updates

questions.question: Explain buffer overflow vulnerability. How does it occur and what programming practices can prevent it?
keyPoints: Buffer overflow writes data beyond allocated memory boundaries
Occurs in languages like C/C++ without bounds checking
Can overwrite adjacent memory, including return addresses
Prevention: use safe string functions (strcpy_s, strncpy)
Use languages with built-in bounds checking
Stack canaries, address space layout randomization (ASLR)
Regular code audits and static analysis tools
